nodes.context: A street gang founded in early 1960s in Chicago, initially Polish, later Hispanic, involved in drug trafficking and violence.
An alliance of street gangs in Chicago, formed during the 1980s.
An alliance of street gangs in Chicago, formed during the 1980s.
A Chicago street gang, part of the Folk Nation.
A Chicago street gang, part of the People Nation.

edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1980s to early 1990s, many Saint gang members were incarcerated within the prison system which led the Saints to join the People Nation for protection.
edges.context: The Saints joined the People Nation for protection in prison.

edges.evidence_quote: They were brought into the People Nation by the Almighty Latin Kings as cousins, hence the addition of 'Almighty' ... and the five-point star.
edges.context: The Latin Kings brought the Saints into the People Nation as cousins.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1998, a 12-year-old boy shot two teen Gangster Disciple members in the Back of the Yards neighborhood, in the hope of joining the Almighty Saints street gang.
edges.context: A boy shot Gangster Disciples members to join the Saints, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation , Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , La Raza Nation , Gangster Two Six , Satan's Disciples , Latin Kings
edges.context: The infobox lists People Nation as a rival.
